# services.lldap

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.lldap`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.lldap.database.createLocally` | `boolean` | Create the database and database user locally. |
| `services.lldap.database.type` | `one of "mariadb", "postgresql", "sqlite"` | Database engine to use. |
| `services.lldap.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable lldap, a lightweight authentication server that provides an opinionated, simplified LDAP interface for authentication. |
| `services.lldap.environment` | `attribute set of string` | Environment variables passed to the service. Any config option name prefixed with `LLDAP_` takes priority over the one in the configuration file. |
| `services.lldap.environmentFile` | `null or absolute path` | Environment file as defined in {manpage}`systemd.exec(5)` passed to the service. |
| `services.lldap.package` | `package` | The lldap package to use. |
| `services.lldap.settings` | `open submodule of (TOML value)` | Free-form settings written directly to the {file}`lldap_config.toml` file. Refer to <https://github.com/lldap/lldap/blob/main/lldap_config.docker_template.toml> for supported values. |
| `services.lldap.settings.database_url` | `null or string` | Database URL. |
| `services.lldap.settings.force_ldap_user_pass_reset` | `boolean or value "always" (singular enum)` | Force reset of the admin password. Set this setting to `"always"` to update the admin password when `ldap_user_pass_file` changes. Setting to `"always"` also means any password update in the UI will be overwritten next time the service restarts. The difference between `true` and `"always"` is the former is intended for a one time fix while the latter is intended for a declarative workflow. In practice, the result is the same: the password gets reset. The only practical difference is the former outputs a warning message while the latter outputs an info message. |
| `services.lldap.settings.http_host` | `string` | The host address that the HTTP server will be bound to. |
| `services.lldap.settings.http_port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port on which to have the HTTP server, for user login and administration. |
| `services.lldap.settings.http_url` | `string` | The public URL of the server, for password reset links. |
| `services.lldap.settings.jwt_secret_file` | `null or string` | Path to a file containing the JWT secret. |
| `services.lldap.settings.ldap_base_dn` | `string` | Base DN for LDAP. |
| `services.lldap.settings.ldap_host` | `string` | The host address that the LDAP server will be bound to. |
| `services.lldap.settings.ldap_port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port on which to have the LDAP server. |
| `services.lldap.settings.ldap_user_dn` | `string` | Admin username |
| `services.lldap.settings.ldap_user_email` | `string` | Admin email. |
| `services.lldap.settings.ldap_user_pass` | `null or string` | Password for default admin password. Unsecure: Use `ldap_user_pass_file` settings instead. |
| `services.lldap.settings.ldap_user_pass_file` | `null or string` | Path to a file containing the default admin password. If you want to update the default admin password through this setting, you must set `force_ldap_user_pass_reset` to `true`. Otherwise changing this setting will have no effect unless this is the very first time LLDAP is started and its database is still empty. |
| `services.lldap.silenceForceUserPassResetWarning` | `boolean` | Disable warning when the admin password is set declaratively with the `ldap_user_pass_file` setting but the `force_ldap_user_pass_reset` is set to `false`. This can lead to the admin password to drift from the one given declaratively. If that is okay for you and you want to silence the warning, set this option to `true`. |
